source: Translated (clean-room) from the Assure Ledger-Scrutiny engine (47 rules) to the ERPNext GL model. Provenance `assure_rule` is the original rule tag. See reference/assure-rules-catalog.md.

contract.note: run_scrutiny(rule_pack, engagement_config, filters) evaluates each ACTIVE rule whose `kind` it supports, resolving $materiality:* bindings from compute_materiality(engagement_config). Each rule is a FROZEN, server-evaluated predicate over the ERPNext GL — never model-emitted SQL. Output per hit: {rule_id, assure_rule, severity, section, effective_date, statement, ref_doctype, ref_name, amount, query, materiality_used}. Rules with status=needs_legal_review are SKIPPED unless engagement_config.include_unreviewed=true, and every statutory finding carries section+effective_date+disclaimer.

kinds.dormant_party_ledger: party (Supplier/Customer) with outstanding balance on `balance_side` but zero GL movement in the period (or in the last half-period when `window=last_half`).
kinds.wrong_side_balance: accounts matching `account_filter` carrying a balance on `wrong_side` above `min_amount`.
kinds.advance_in_party: supplier debit balance (advance paid) or customer credit balance (advance received) above `min_amount`.
kinds.cash_txn_over_threshold: GL entries whose voucher has a cash/bank counter-leg, on the `account_filter` group, `direction` payment/receipt, single-voucher amount over `threshold` (STATUTORY).
kinds.voucher_pct_of_ledger: a single voucher whose amount is >= `pct` of the account's period turnover, on `root_type`, above `min_amount`.
kinds.sign_flip_yoy: account whose closing-balance sign differs from the prior fiscal year's, magnitude above `min_amount`.
kinds.tb_balance_check: period trial balance: sum(debit) must equal sum(credit) at `tolerance_dp` decimal places (opening and closing).
kinds.msme_overdue: MSME-registered supplier payables outstanding beyond `days` (STATUTORY 43B(h)). Per (party, voucher) net credit from Payable-account GL, aged from the earliest credit posting; the MSME detection basis (Supplier field vs supplier_group fallback) is stated in each hit note.
kinds.expense_variance_yoy: per Expense leaf account: ratio-to-revenue (Income root_type total) CY vs the prior Fiscal Year; flag when the ratio's percent change `(cy_ratio/py_ratio)*100-100` is >= `variance_pct` in magnitude AND the CY amount >= `min_amount`. Not evaluable without a prior FY, PY GL rows and nonzero revenue in both periods.
kinds.pl_yoy_swing: per P&L leaf account (Income/Expense): natural-side period activity, flag when |CY-PY| / max(|PY|,1) >= `swing_pct`% AND |CY-PY| >= `min_amount`, PY = the prior Fiscal Year. Not evaluable without a prior FY and PY GL rows.

rules.statement: Sundry creditor balance with no transactions during the year.
Sundry creditor balance with no transactions during the last six months.
Sundry creditor with a credit balance above materiality and no payment during the year.
Sundry debtor balance with no transactions during the year.
Advance received from a customer above materiality (credit balance in a receivable ledger).
Advance paid to a supplier above materiality (debit balance in a payable ledger).
Debit balance under a borrowing / term-loan account.
Debit-balance ledger under the Revenue group, above P&L materiality.
Credit-balance ledger under the Expense group, above P&L materiality.
A single voucher exceeds 50% of its income/expense ledger balance (above materiality).
A single voucher is between 30% and 50% of its income/expense ledger balance (above materiality).
A ledger's closing-balance side flipped versus the prior year (debit<->credit).
Trial balance must balance: sum of debits equals sum of credits (opening and closing) to 1 decimal place.
s.40A(3): cash payment towards an expense exceeding the single-payment limit is disallowable.
rules.disclaimer: Threshold and the transporter (s.40A(3A)) carve-out change by year; verify current law before relying on this finding. As coded: single cash payment > 10,000; the 35,000 transporter exception is NOT modelled.

rules.statement: s.40A(3): cash payment to a creditor exceeding the single-payment limit is disallowable.
rules.disclaimer: Verify current-year limit and the 35,000 transporter carve-out (not modelled). As coded: single cash payment > 10,000 against a Payable account.

rules.statement: s.269ST: cash receipt at/above the aggregate limit attracts penalty.
rules.disclaimer: s.269ST 2,00,000 limit and its per-person/per-transaction/per-occasion tests are NOT fully modelled here; this flags single cash receipts >= 200000 against a Receivable account as a review prompt only. Verify current law.

rules.statement: MSME supplier dues outstanding beyond the statutory window (s.43B(h) disallowance).
rules.disclaimer: The 15/45-day window depends on a written agreement; assure does not hardcode it. As coded: per (supplier, voucher) net credit outstanding on Payable accounts, aged from the earliest credit posting, overdue > 45 days. MSME detection basis: the deployed Supplier schema has NO MSME field (verified: stock schema, no is_msme/msme_status/msme_registration_number/udyam fields), so detection FALLS BACK to Supplier Group name containing 'msme'; if such a field is later added, the engine uses it automatically and each hit note states the basis actually used. Verify the applicable window and MSME registration per supplier.

rules.statement: Expense account's ratio-to-revenue moved sharply versus the prior year (YoY analytical review).
P&L account's period activity swung sharply versus the prior year.

legal_review.scope: All 4 statutory rules activated on founder approval for FY 2026-2027 (dev). Known modelling gaps remain documented per-rule in `disclaimer` (40A(3A) transporter carve-out, 269ST aggregation tests, MSME 15/45-day window).
